reviews.review_description:
Ready, trial looks good. 12,99$ for nxt chapter. It's ok. It looks brilliant. This is one of the scenes why I bought my metaquest3.

Please change language to german, it must be possible to switch between american and german.

Thanks for this little diamond.

Some guys told me it looks like Final Fantasy, why not, if it runs good. This is a big merchandise.

Episode 2 runs good. It looks good on my Quest3 Headset.

Thanks again for this little diamond.

Waiting for Episode 3.

I wish it was full 360 VR and not 180° but overall, it was really well made, looking forward to the next episode!

Episode 2 is even better than Ep1 however theres still only 1 interactive scene just like Ep1. Im not understanding why they even bothered adding these interactive scenes if theres only 1 per episode. Just make it a movie and get rid of these parts as they really dont add anything and they are pretty much the same in both episodes.

The graphics are great, should have been a video game. 

But, the stories are too short and the interaction is limited. 

The price you’re charging per episode’s is too expensive. You can buy a complete movie or at least rent one after paying for one or two episodes.

I will not be going further with this story.

I thought this was a video game not a video it didn't say anything about it being a video or video game switches are bad and the graphics aren't that great they do speak a different language in some cutscenes but overall not worth my storage
did a really good job

I really enjoyed first episodes. It's well made, immersive and engaging. I would like to watch the second episode but 10 euro are the price I pay for a month of a streaming service. Too much for 30 minutes of entertainment..

This reminded me very much of the feeling I got when I played metal gear solid for the first time. The technology is there and the art work is there but if course the mechanics are limited. The experience is short and this is going to be a multi series thing which makes sense. Overall I likedthe experience, the story is interesting, the only real thing is that the scale of the characters feel off to me. They like like children but perhaps this is something on my calibration. I won’t give it a 4 start because this is just the first part and for the first I kind of expected more. But the 3 is well earned.

reviews.review_title: Cyber Magnolia, an immersive VR legend!
reviews.review_description: A futuristic Mulan adventure awaits! The graphics are stunning, the combat is smooth, and the immersion is overwhelming. The AI interaction and plot design are eye-opening, and the VR experience is truly breathtaking. Highly recommended!

The concept is amazing but the execution needs a bit of work. Needs just a touch more interactive moments to fully drive the immersion. And I’m not a fan of the “episodes” format but I feel once the kinks have been worked out it could possibly be an amazing experience.
Quite interesting but why does it require so much storage? Too expensive for Indian users.

The visual quality is stunning! The single-eye 4K resolution is incredibly sharp in VR. The details of the futuristic city and the character's skin textures are all crystal clear—it's a truly cinematic experience. The moment I put on the headset, I was transported to 2125.

Attempting an interactive movie game in VR is a brilliant idea with huge potential! The experience so far is great; the scenes, characters, and world-building are all very detailed. I can really feel the effort the developers put into this.I'm curious, are there plans for multiple endings in the future? That would make the story even more captivating! Really looking forward to what you do next!

I was given a free copy of the first episode for testing and feedback.

So, what is this? Is it a game? Not really. It has an interactive section that has no impact on the outcome. Maybe the other episodes do more, but I didn't see those.

Is it a VR movie? Pretty much. They need to accept that and respect basic video controls. At least let me pause, but I would also want some kind of fast forward or skip. I had to stop watching because life happens, and I couldn't pause. When I went back to it I had to start over. It's not that long, so it's not that huge an issue, but it's silly to leave it out.

Also, there are limitations to the medium. As with other 3d videos I've seen, you don't want to get things too close to the camera. When you do, at least for me, I get double vision. I assume it's not just me, though. Then because it's weird you try to focus on it, which you can't do. It doesn't happen a lot, but you have a done companion, and it's sometimes an issue.

So, it's a movie with a bit of interaction. How is it? I actually rather like it, at least the first episode. It's a scifi CGI movie and that's extra points in my world. If I was just reading the movie, it would be 4 stars. The 3d is cool, even with issues. The plot is interesting and I want to know more. The quality of the 3d video is actually quite good and it's pretty well animated and detailed. There's some rather obvious pandering, but I'm used to that. The interactive section was actually quite fun, even if it had no impact on the outcome, it still helped with engagement. 

Unfortunately, as a movie with a built in video player with no controls, I have to take off a star. That's what early access is about, though. I hope they fix this before the final release because I want to see the rest.

Is an semi interactive,cinematic experience that had high quality renders that you would expect from a Final Fantasy game.

The first episode pretty much lay down the premise of the world and environment laying just enough questions that make you wanting more.

The wait for the following episodes is going to be a painful wait.

The interactivity is a further unexpected suprise in a genre that isn't really represented in VR often (the cinematic experience)

It really paints the way forward for a genre that I would like to see expanded.
Its too early to tell if the developer are going to go down the route of diverging story lines depends on your choices you make in the interactive scenes.
But nevertheless Mulan 2125 isn't scared to champion an genre that gets very little representation and still defies its conventions (and thus gets extra respect from me)

Im looking forward to see how the story ends and how the developer build on the the groundwork laid here in future releases

developer_response.body:
Appreciate the thoughtful review! Sounds like you’re saving that last star for the plot twist in Episode 2.
Don’t worry — we’re working hard to earn the full constellation.

reviews.review_description:
Rating: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️☆ (4/5)

What I liked:
- The app is easy to install and runs smoothly.  
- The experience feels intuitive, with a clean and user-friendly interface.  
- Graphics and design are well done, and overall performance is solid.  
- It delivers what it promises — fun and useful features.

What could be better:
- Some instructions or menus are a bit unclear at first.  
- More customization options would be nice.  
- Minor bugs or rough edges in some areas.

Overall impression:
This app is definitely worth trying. If you're into VR experiences and looking for something new, it’s a solid choice. While not perfect, it offers a lot and works reliably. I’d give it a strong 4 out of 5 stars.

I gotta say that this is a first for me, watching short stories in vr while having some interactives, at least I hope there's more after episode 1. The graphic tho needs a major improvement as everything looks blurry to me. For someone who's got a strong vr leg, this makes me dizzy still at certain parts and the 2D is a no no for me especially with the boarders on the sides being super annoying and ruins immersion. Overall this is not for me at all but if ur new and looking for something different, u might find it more entertaining than I did.

reviews.review_title: This CAN be great, but it's not yet.
reviews.review_description:
I did enjoy my time with this experience. However, after playing something like Human Within, it makes this one feel much emptier. While Human Within was with humans and this is CGI, there's still a bit of a disconnect. Some of the animations are SUPER exagerated. I do not like some of the Voice Acting. The lines are delivered very wooden. 

Other than that, I think the setting was pretty cool and it has the potential to be something really special.

This review only concerns episode 1.
To begin with, this app is not a game , but rather a 3D, 180° film with a few rare interactions, but this does not detract from the quality of the work. 
This episode takes place in the future and puts us in the shoes of the character Mulan. This first part allows us to learn a little about the different characters and sets the stage for the action to begin... And the episode ends, leaving us eager to know what happens next.
Technically, it's a success, the characters are well rendered and the environments (especially in the second part) are very immersive.
My only criticism is the lack of subtitles for people who have difficulty with English.
In any case, I'm eagerly awaiting the next episode.

This is a 3D 180 movie, the graphics are awesome and I'd like to think that this is a great new way to watch animation in the future, but being split into 2 parts and being made to wait so long for a second instalment... thats a NO NO.
The story isn't anything ground breaking, its just 'OK', and the acting of the main character leaves a lot to be desired.
I don't hate it.... but I don't love it either, it's needs to be released as one full movie or series, but it's a great glimpse into what might become in the future for watching animation in VR headsets.

reviews.review_title: The Dolby Atmos effect is mind-blowing!
reviews.review_description: The sound comes from all directions—the clashing of metal during battles, the subtle ambient noises—it all gave me goosebumps. This kind of all-encompassing sensory immersion is something regular cinemas simply can't provide.

First of all, unlike many that complained about the lack of interactivity of this title, I see no issues with it being a more movie-like experience. Sometimes I just want to sit back and get immersed in a sci-fi story, and this title mostly delivered that. 

I was impressed with the opening scenes, the environment design, the vehicle design, the atmosphere and the character design. Art direction is solid and in high quality. However the dialogue and the scripts leave more to be desired -- being a Chinese production I mostly blame their translation and writing style.

Meanwhile there is a.lot of actions in this title that would be morecentertaining if the camera can be placed smarter to film the shot from a cool angle, instead of slowly panning from the ground to reach there which kinda breaks the immersion.

Overall I want to see kore productions like this one. Knowing that China has poured a lot of money into the VR media initiative I expect more gems to show up on the quest store.

I found the actual experience to be enjoyable and enjoyed what I was ABLE to experience well enough.

I received a key to test and give feedback I don't think the price is worth the experience, but I do like the idea.

The technical side of things is what keeps me from giving this a higher score.

Unlike any other application on my headset and no matter how many times and ways I try to fix it, after testing the first chapter, I was unable to ever play it again. I tried reinstalls. I tried clearing extra room (which I had plenty of already) on the headset. I tried so many things and was never able to get it to work again.

Episode 2 is 🔥🔥🔥
 i hope this inspires more people to make more like this!!!

The First Episode starts off pretty good with nice Visuals, even though they are very blurry sometimes. 
I only saw Episode 1 so far, wich is ok, but nothing too special. 
The first Episode is 30 Minutes long, and could use a little better pacing. 
Even though, the Voice Acting is very amateurish in Parts, i did enjoy it though.

This is much more an experience with some interaction for the first episode, though this may increase in future episodes.

The graphics are something special for a standalone vr experience. The different environments, characters and effects have clearly had a lot of care and effort put into them. There is still an essence of uncanny valley here and there with the characters, but it's generally impressive throughout.

The storytelling takes a bit of time to get going, and the first episode is a lot of scene setting, but there's plenty of hooks and cliffhangers to keep the suspense between episodes.

There are one or two scenes early on where it felt a bit discomforting due to the camera movement, but overall it is a mild VR experience.

Overall, it's worth checking out and I'm intrigued for the next episodes and what more they can achieve.

reviews.review_title: Great concept, just needs more content
reviews.review_description:
This is essentially watching a animated show in vr. The animations look great, though at times it can be very blurry. There is a small part where you have to guide a drone/camera to spot enemies so the character can find a route to escape. That's all the interaction there is. So just be prepared to just watch a show.

With that being said, I dont think it's worth buying because you can easily just find free high quality videos on YouTube to watch.

To justify the price, they would need to add more interactivity, maybe making it into something similar to the Taletale games. Giving you dialog choices and options for where to take the story.

developer_response.body: sorry that it didn't meet your expectation

reviews.review_description:
OVERVIEW
This is more of a semi-interactive cinematic VR experience than a traditional game.
You mostly watch, with a few moments of interaction, so it feels closer to a 3D/180° film set in a sci-fi world.
-
VISUALS & ANIMATION
Character models look fantastic, with detailed faces and very fluid animation.
Facial expressions and body movement sell emotions well and are among the best parts of the experience.
Environments are nicely stylized and give a good sense of being on a ship, though some areas feel a bit too big and empty and certain background details don’t fully match how characters behave.
-
AUDIO
Dialogue is clear and easy to follow.
Sound effects are strong and add a lot of immersion without overpowering voices.
Overall audio feels polished and cinematic.
-
STORY & CHARACTERS
The episode works well as an introduction and raises interesting questions about the world and its characters.
Mulan’s frustration and emotional reactions make her engaging, but some character motivations still feel unclear at this stage.
It’s easy to see potential for deeper worldbuilding, but it needs to unfold at a good pace to stay compelling.
-
INTERACTIVITY & IMMERSION
The transition from the “movie” segment into a more interactive drone-style sequence is very smooth and impressive.
However, the lack of meaningful control (for example the drone moving on its own) feels like a missed opportunity.
The inverted half-sphere “screen” that moves with your head can break immersion; a more grounded or stationary presentation would help.
-
OVERALL
A visually impressive, well-animated and great-sounding VR cinematic with limited interactivity, strong technical polish, and promising but still mysterious storytelling.
Can’t wait for part 3!!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi there, it would be great if you could tell us some details about what's happening.
Update:
Thank you so much for the support!

reviews.review_description:
I was provided a game key for testing the first part of a three-part experience. 
Mulan 2125 is a very good-looking interactive 180 degrees experience. The story was more like 
watching a movie with 
some interaction towards the end of the 
episode, but I wish there was more chances to interact with the story. I felt that the experience 
was a bit short but left me intrigued about what the next chapters will bring

Maybe it could be an interesting game but they threw VR animated movie out the window and just made a low quality, low bitrate 180º video with very few gameplay gimmicks. This style of video is only popular in porn. This is just not good

developer_response.body: It's a film app in the entertainment genre. We have done everything we could on this platform to label it and marketing it as a film. It's an irrelevant review from a gamer's point of view.

reviews.review_description: Had to call it a game as it was more like watching a movie. Graphics were good, and felt invested in the story. Wish it had a 360 view instead of the 180 flat screen view. Would’ve been cool to have that as it would’ve been much more immersive.

Very nice graphics and 3d effect.
Pretty good story in the first of three episodes. Interested to see the rest.
Bit of interactivity at the end if the episode was fun, hope to see more in the rest of the episodes.
Bit short for non sale price but seems they are considering price points for further episodes.

totally captivating with great graphics that grip you the whole way through. the brief deliance with interplay at the end was quite the surprise as was the counteractive lies coming from both sides. will stay tuned for part two and give this five stars.

Its very cinematic and entertaining on where this story line is going. The detail especially interesting, it reminds me when I got the first meta quest and it came with a little 5 minuite animation on showing how amazing VR can be. I look foward to seeing more video like applications where they are also interactive!

One of the first in this niche which of course means the prices do not quite align with what would be expected in a more competitive category. The movie was enjoyable, the on-rails portion toward the end of the episode felt a little interactive but some limitations in the CGI technology meant some of the more expressive and heartfelt portions fell a little flat.

The plot seems fairly good but unfortunately ends on a cliffhanger and also may not have set up enough context clues to get excited about the next episode. All I know is the main character possesses something that feels like they've grown up on a ship in the middle of nowhere to keep secret and it's probably tied to the giant bomb at the very start.

It does make me excited and open for the possibilities in this category, I can see how the choose your own adventure or visual novel experience would actually work in the VR space!

So I saw this first episode as a “play test” item so I did not have to pay for it. Although to be honest, I probably would, just for the unique experience it provides. It’s not a “game” but it is an experience.

I really enjoyed the visuals, and overall production quality of this immersive 3D VR movie. It looks great, really pulls you into the world and it’s easy to forget you’re just watching from a headset. I love that it’s presented as a VR experience rather than a standard flatscreen film, as the sense of presence is done really done well. I have to applaud the developers because there should be more of this type of thing in VR.

That said, I was hoping for a little more interactivity. There’s some small interaction towards the end but aside from that, it’s mostly just watching the story unfold around you. It kind of reminded me of those “choose your own adventure” books I used to read as a kid. You know where you finish a chapter and then it would give you three different choices and you would choose one of them and whichever one you chose would lead you on a different journey. It had those throughout the book. I would’ve loved to see that kind of branching story design here. If not only for continued interaction but also for replay-ability and more interesting stories as well.

I would recommend this for people who have an interest in VR cinema and what’s possible therein. Not sure what they’re landing on for cost but at this point, I’d say keep an eye on this and the progress in the Meta store and maybe pick it up if it peaks your interest!

The opening scene of this theatrical narrative was like watching a movie, and I see alot of potential in this game.  It is unlike most games in that it is almost like you are a character in a movie and the attempt is to totally immerse you in the experience.  There are some details that could use a little work, but I still am looking forward to the next chapter and hope there is more hands on activity rather than just watching some scenes unfold.   
As I said, "playing" the game, which meant watching for the most part, was like seeing a movie, if you looked to the right or left then you were looking beyond the movie screen at just darkness.  It is a 180 degree experience so do not expect to turn around and see anything going on behind you.  I do appreciate what the developers are trying to do with this and only hope later episodes become more interactive and that the story starts making more sense and unfolding.
Step into a post-apocalyptic future, follow Mulan’s journey through betrayal and survival in this immersive episodic VR adventure. Dramatic visuals, emotional story, VR storytelling redefined.

reviews.review_title: An experience you'll want more of
reviews.review_description:
Mulan2125  is a cinematic delight that combines sci-fi with history. The world feels very real and immersive. The sound quality and effects are super well done. I enjoy this genre and have several titles in my library.  For every one of them, I have had to wear ear buds to be fully engaged and to clearly hear dialogue.  I never had to use them here, and I deeply appreciate that. 
The interaction with the cinimatcs works perfectly, and the story leaves you wanting more. If you're reading this and enjoy interactive stories as much as I do, this is a great one to add to your library.

This experience is different from what you have most likely experienced in virtual reality. This opened up a new look for uses of VR. I loved the graphics and animations of the game. The audio was pretty good. My only wish is that the experience was more interactive. I would 9/10 recommend. I hope for the best of this experience!
Minus the minor issues and hiccups along the way the story is honestly really interesting and compelling. I found myself wanting to watch more, wanting to find out what happened next

I didn't go into this with any expectations. I was pleasantly suprised.

The experience is more akin to an IMax movie, a huge screen curved in front of you with little interaction. If you are looking for a super interactice experience akin to a game, this is not for you. However, if you like scifi and worldbuilding this might be right up your alley.

As for critiques, I found myself trying to look at people who walked off screen and was dissapointed as it is a fixed camera. I also think that it would be interesting to have some more interaction with the world and be able to see things up closer. That leans more into game territory which this isn't so that is more personal preference than a dig at the experience.

Overall, good low intensity way to show what VR is capable of. Worth the price of admission I'd say. It would do well with some popcorn.

reviews.review_title: Expect a movie not a game.
reviews.review_description: This review is based on early access to episode 1. Expect more of a movie experience than a game. Mulan really puts you into the story with its fly on the wall perspective thanks to the 180 degree field of view. This experience is low on interactivity but high on immersion. The first episode has to do a lot of heavy story lifting. It has to do world and character building. The character and world visuals are well done. We should expect future installments to up the ante and viewer engagement.
Thanks you for responding to my review and explaining looking forward to seeing how it develops  keep up the good work 👍 have a got any idea when the next episode will be available

In my movie reviewing, I would never judge a flick without watching it from beginning to end, so bear with my truncated notes. I was given the chance to evaluate the first episode of Mulan 2125 in a closed testing environment, so here is my review. If I finish the entire series, I'll update accordingly.
 
Glad to see a studio is taking some chances with new ideas, such as this pseudo-interactive movie. It does look nice, and the story is intriguing, although I did catch some graphical issues. 
- Soft around edges
- Clear 180-degree viewing window, scenes force you to look left/right, exposing this
- Frame rate seems locked, causing occasional ghosting

Overall, if more interactivity is added, and maybe shined up a bit, this could really be something exciting. Plus, the interaction HAS to matter; the single scene in this first episode is on rails. 3.5/5

reviews.review_title: Nice to see more VR pushing boundaries
reviews.review_description: I love that this game is trying new things with the concept of VR and interactive media. The story and world are intriguing. The graphics look stellar. Definitely worth checking out.

The experience is worthwhile in itself: the 180-degree 3D, the interactivity, the perfectly spatialized sound—all of this makes me say it's a good experience, even if one can I found it a bit short And a little too close to a 180p 3D film, but as they say, someone has to jump into the fray. For me, all interactive films/games should be like this, but of course, like Many of us would like this kind of experience to be longer, in any case, congratulations to the whole team for your work and good luck for the future.

I can appreciate what Mulan 2125 is striving towards with their first episode. A cinematic experience with excellent graphics and sound that really pulls you into the experience. While there's not a whole lot of interactivity in the first episode; if you go into it expecting that then you're more likely to enjoy it at least speaking fro my own experience. I hope the developers are able to push the boundaries and deliver more interactivity. Even if its not technically possible... even having branching sections where decisions may affect pre-rendered video might go a long way to push the interactivity. Either way I'm looking forward to see where Mulan's adventure takes her.

I'm writing this from the perspective of someone that never 'watched' a VR movie like that. I have watched the episode one for free. 
Starting up, it is rather interesting. The story is more or less standard, there is some postapo, some bad guys, some good guys, just standard cookie cutter. The graphics are rendered in real time, and they aren't bad - I'd say better then gta IV, so nothing terrible, but nothing great. Better than a lot of quest games tho. As for interactivity it's not really there, at least in the episode one - just a single action sequence in the whole episode. Overall, it's a nice experience - at least once, to see how it looks and perhaps if it sticks.

This game is incredible in terms of graphics but it feels more of a graphics demo then anything else. The story is extremely disappointing and the constant pay walls feel impeding. The game is meant to feel new; however, I found the game tedious to get through and very unoriginal. The graphics are generally good, but everything else isn't pushed enough to really shine. Although I liked the game somewhat, it isn't worth the money and feels cheap, bland, and uninspired.

reviews.review_title: A Movie Experience in a VR Gaming World
reviews.review_description: I experienced the first episode of Mulan early on in release as a playtester. They definitely had some cool visuals along with issues that caused the eye strain. Fortunately, the developer worked quickly to resolve the issues causing discomfort and increased the clarity of the visuals in the process. This app was a new and novel experience apart from my previous adventures in VR. The storytelling reminded me of an anime and I'm excited to see where they take it. At the time of my review, the majority of the cost is per episode, but I believe the developer is still hashing this out. If you are looking to try something new in VR this may be the app for you.

reviews.review_title: Pretty good for what it is
reviews.review_description: Mulan is a pretty good interactive show. Everything looks pretty good and is immersive. The story itself is interesting and at the end of episode 1 had me wanting to watch more. I kind of wish you were able to see 360 since you can only see 180 but if you can get past that it’s not a big deal. I wish there were more interactive parts as there was only one interactive part in episode one. I like how it is cinematic but some of the camera angles at moments are kinda weird and threw me off. Overall this game is pretty good for an interactive vr show and can’t wait to see the rest of the story unfold.

reviews.review_title: Great graphics for a narrative game
reviews.review_description:
Graphic is high top for a VR narrative game however interaction is minimal ( making the movie more interactive would increase longevity in my opinion)with still some polishing required. It's a great experience if you want to try  VR movies at 180degrees.
Also the story itself is quite interesting and for mature audience.

reviews.review_title: Not bad, not using its full potential
reviews.review_description:
Ive only watched episode 1, so that opinion might not be valid for the next episodes, but i think this series doesnt use the potential of VR enough to justify its drawbacks.
I wont go into anything about the story, just how the watching experience is.
It is mainly a 180° 3D video. There is no moving and barely looking around. Due to it being a VR experience they had to stay with more static cameras. The interactions it promises, while it is a nice to have compared to most VR media not offering anything, is kept to a amall section in the episode.
If you really want the VR video experience, it might be a good choice, but personally i think this is holding it back too much

This game looks awesome. The graphics look really good when you get used to the blur. It's hard to get used to the blur at first because it looks just like DLSS on pc.

The story is alright, but the thing that bothers me the most is that there aren't enough interactions in the game.

This is a 3d movie in 180degrees.
It's nice to see the genre and storytelling pushing forward and trying new things. It's worth it to check out even if it's not infinitely replayable.

I look forward to see how this space develops and what the developer can do with this format.
The cinamatics are amazing it's like you're inside  a 3D movie but the only downside is a lack of a pause features, other than that it's great.

reviews.review_title: Nice Take on a Visual Novel
reviews.review_description: This is a nice visual novel. If you go in with that in mind it, you will let go of the notions of extensive gameplay. At least Ep. 1 is framed that way. There isn't much interaction but some world building, which gives a good Sci-fi thriller vibe. There are issues with particle effects have inconsistencies between eyes, but that doesn't take a lot away from the narrative. Overall it's a good showing with some technical issues.

It's a cinematic movie done in full 180 degrees. It really is captivating in that, more than the traditional 3D films on screen. I like the world they built, and I like the characters. Where it kind of stumbles is the resolution. Having to fill all that space costs them some pixels. It's pretty ok, but it's not as crisp as a regular 3D movie. Also the price feels a bit high at $10 per part. Try out the free episode (it's much shorter than a regular episode).

reviews.review_title: An interesting attempt to expand VR
reviews.review_description:
I was lucky enough to be selected to test this and was intrigued by what the developers were attempting in the vr space.

To be up front, this is not really a game. This is more a proof of concept for virtual cinema/TV with interactive elements. While much of it may be static viewpoints, being able to watch it play out like a fly on the wall and frame the scene based on where you look an a 180 degree field of view shows how things could be moving forward as the world of technology progresses. 

The graphics and animation were nice enough considering the team size, fluid with no issues on a Q3, and the scope of the project. Sound design did the job although some voice lines were a little hard to decipher in one particular scene and most of the supporting casts voice acting was quality enough to allow you to enjoy the ride. Unfortunately the voice actor for Mulan herself was somewhat jarring with the delivery not matching the tone of the scene at times.

The story moves and is paced at a nice rate for an introduction and rising escalation for a first episode and leaves off at a nice cliff hanger where wanting to see what occurs in the future with the one main interactive section at the end providing a little insight as to how interactive VR media could evolve over time to add some engagement to whatever you may be watching in the future.

All in all, an interesting experiment which has left me curious to see how the remaining episodes unfold and how the idea of interactive cinema can be expanded upon in future releases.

Worth a try if such a prospect intrigues you and you are okay with the idea of this not really being a game but a look into the world of user controlled interactive story based media.

I liked it! There is one full episode and a free trial. I watched the full episode, and it's good it gives a different perspective on media in VR, and it looked great. Like a final fantasy movie, but you felt like you could touch the scenery in the movie.

I think they are pushing it a bit charging £13 for one 30 min episode. I got it on discount, so I only paid £4 in total. It should be around £6 i think anything more than that and you have to bite the bullet.

But I look forward to the next episode and I hope the dev makes more of these kinds games in future.

reviews.review_title: Sitting down is wise for this wild ride
reviews.review_description: This is a story-driven experience that take us for a wild ride.  The visuals are captivating (kind of a blend of holigraphic with 3D, but in a view that feels more intimate than a 180 view), and the sound is immersive.  With the first epiosde being the main release at the moment, the use of handtracking was not until deep into the episode, but when presented a fun perspective that I wish was afforded to the whole episode (ambitious, I know).   I did not really know what to expect or when to expect it and so my rating here is because I would have prefer internal controls for the cinematic pieces when the hand tracking was not in use.  Was glad to see an accompanying trailer.  I found the FAQ very helpful as well.  Hopefully we get to interact even more in the next episode.

This looks promising, but will probably wait for all episodes to release.
Is the visual fidelity of the trial the same as in the episode I wonder? Because it seems to be a bit grainy with some artifacts and frame rate issues. Hopefully the main episodes are the best they can be. 
I believe that most people are ok with downloading a huge file for the movie. Developers should provide the biggest and best file possible with the highest quality image.

This was somewhat unexpected. It’s not really a VR game. More of an experience. The story was quite engaging. There were some issues with being able to focus clearly on certain areas of the output and also because the video is only in a 180° circle if you turned your head too far it would break immersion a little bit but on whole it’s actually quite good.￼

Not a bad introduction to what could be an overall decent story...so the story was decent, the graphics were pretty good, although the resolution seemed a bit low and there were a few times I noticed some minimal clipping but overall not bad...the sound was over all good, but the voice acting at times left a bit to be desired and the English dubbing didn't sync with the mouth movements so it always seemed a little off...but the most lacking part of this was the interactivity, when starting it tells you to have a clean and safe play space and warns you your arms may even get tired, only for the only interactive portion of the entire first episode feel not only underwhelming, but only lasted maybe a minute if that and only involved pointing your controller on certain directions for a few seconds... overall not a bad cinematic experience, but don't expect much more than that, at least in episode one...
I haven't figured out if this is a game to me it feels like watching a small movie and that ain't what I paid for so far I am not impressed

reviews.review_title: A different way to experience a story
reviews.review_description: For now I have only seen the first episode of Mulan 2125, and it's very clear that the first episode is for establishing the setting and the characters. The futuristic tech and mystery are in the air as we don't yet get to experience the twist or understand the power of the protagonist.  An important thing to note is that this is more of a movie than a game (so far) and there is only one section that requires direct input from you. Enjoy it like a movie, figure out and enjoy the story

reviews.review_title: I like what they are doing
reviews.review_description: It's like being a part of the movie, loved it, feels more personal. It's an interesting Idea and I wish we saw more of this in VR. I look forward to seeing the next episodes. Hoping for more interactions in the upcoming episodes.

​The tech behind these visuals is impressive. It’s one of the few VR experiences that actually feels High-Definition. The cyberpunk art style is vibrant and sharp, with zero blur. The narrative pacing is masterfully handled, keeping the tension high throughout. The interaction is light but meaningful, serving the story perfectly. A visual feast for the eyes!

This review is for episode 1. I haven't ever watched an immersive story on my Quest before. Mulan is a pleasant surprise. The first episode lays out the back story of the environment,  what it is like in the future and the characters. It is a great visual and audio cinematic experience that is done in  180 degree experience. The first episode sucks you right in from the start and keeps you immersed through to the end. There is just enough intrigue to make you want to watch the next episode. Close to the end of the episode there is an interactive section that was a big surprise to me. I really enjoyed this episode 1 and can't wait for episode 2.

reviews.review_title: Wow, i was engaged the whole time
reviews.review_description: Unbelievable, being totally immersed in the story like that was mind blowing the graphics are really great the story is really good, great job developers

Overall is a great semi 3D interactive with fun charecters and story plot but overall stumbles when it comes with pkayer interaction. This story about a girl in her birthday is attack and abstracted by the enemy fighting for her way out to find her father is a love project by its developers. They took the time and care into this project but I just wish there was more for the player to do. 

But overall is a great watch and with high detail with visuals and sounds its a fun new story id love to see more of this univer

reviews.review_title: Much more of what I would expect of a vr
reviews.review_description: Majority of vr games I have seen are under whelming. The story rich nature of the game is amazing. It is very short, but that's to be expected, being so story rich. Hopefully this company will go on to be the next Quantic Dream for vr.

Disclaimer: I got early access to Episode 1 as part of a closed playtest

The Visuals are some of the best I have seen in VR so far.  The futuristic story is pretty interesting with some great potential. It's mostly a 180° movie which is a very intriguing concept I haven't seen before. It takes a bit to get used to it not being 360° but once I did it felt very immersive. Though the immersion of some scenes was hurt a little by the limited perspective.

I feel like in some scenes the Immersion was also hurt by the camera moving a bit too fast and/or jumping around too frequently, forcing me to refocus. 

Where the experience sadly falls short is the Interactive part : only is a very short scene at the end is actually interactive. I think the episode would be a lot better if had a few more interactive segments. Also, the Interactive scene that the game only gives you very limited control and is so easy that you seemingly complete it no matter what you do. 

Though it looks like the Future episodes might be lot more interactive which is very exciting.

reviews.review_title: The Future of VR Story Telling
reviews.review_description: Mulan 2125 is a really cool cinematic experience that tries something new in VR. It’s basically a 180-degree episodic movie that delivers a visually stunning and interesting story. What you get is a 3D CGI film with a few interactive scenes that make you feel like you’re part of the action. Since it’s episodic, the ending leaves you wanting more. It’s an awesome change of pace and a great addition for any Quest owner.

This isn't your typical game it's an immersive movie and it drops you directly into the heart of the story right from the get-go

Episode 1 was a short 30 minute 180⁰ cinematic experience with impressive visuals and audio, it will be interesting to see if Quest games optimizer will enhance it as I noticed a little aliaising in certain parts. Additional haptics wouldn't go amiss either I definitely think it would help enhance the immersion 

I'm still a little confused about what's going on but definitely intrigued enough to keep watching, so roll on episode 2!

Mulan 2125 is a good cinamatic experience that i liked watching and i like how its not just a flat screen and you can actualy look around with a 180 dergree view but it was lacking a bit in the intaractive part in the first episode there was only one small intaractive part where you didnt get to do much

As a VR movie enthusiast, I appreciate the concept and ambition behind making the cinematic experience more interactive. The storyline and emotional depth of the characters stood out most, as I genuinely felt their tension, concern, and especially enjoyed their sass and sarcasm. The writing was spot-on and believable. The direct, fourth-wall-breaking moments (when they speak facing the viewer) were when I felt most engaged and fully immersed.

The opening sequence captured my attention immediately as we floated over a dark, stormy sea with titles in the skyline. The artistry and design showed clear attention to detail from the moment we boarded the vessel, mid-crisis. While darting my eyes around the scene, I noticed minor frame-rate stutters and occasional lip-sync mismatches, but neither pulled me out of the story. I would caution this may not be suitable for users sensitive to quick perspective changes, flashing lights, or motion sickness, though I was able to complete episode 1 without any ill effects.

The interactive portion was a nice change compared to other immersive cinematic experiences, giving me a semi-active role in the storyline. It did feel semi-automated, but that didn’t take away from my enjoyment; I liked knowing my participation was mostly optional.

Any of the above technical critiques didn’t pull me too far out of the immersion because the storyline was intriguing enough that I wanted to see what happens next. With each environment change, I found myself scanning every corner, partly because I loved the detailing of the periphery/walls.

I believe the technical aspects are still being refined, so I imagine the experience will continue to improve. I’m looking forward to seeing how the story progresses, as episode 1 leaves plenty of questions unanswered. My only concern is keeping future episodes affordable so the full storyline stays within my reach. I’m grateful I had the chance to view this first episode in exchange for my honest feedback.

In episode 1 you are watching 180 video with a small mini game in the final part. Honestly, i do not see how this will be more immersive than watching as a flat-screen movie. In most vr movie experience like vader immortal or the wolves in the wall, you will roleplay as a character, which gives you the full experience as if you are part of the story/movie. But not this. You will be watching from outside, following camera pan/dolly shot /transition techniques that is usually use in all flat movies.

Secondly, the dubbing, I'd prefer to listen to  chinese dubbing with English subtitles than english dub by non English native speaking actors. I think the actors can do better that way and will be able to express their emotion best in their native language than trying to emulate a language they are not familiar with.

For the story itself, i cant judge from watching just episode 1.the show ended before i can make a judgment if it is exciting or not.

Full disclosure: I was provided a copy of this experience for free to view and review.

Bottom Line: Mulan 2125 showcases some impressive visuals and even has a single flickering interactive moment that tantalizes the possibility of a new type of interactive film in the VR future, but the UI issues, privacy concerns, and incomplete story (as of this review) make this a difficult project to recommend for anyone beyond diehard fans of this niche market.

Overview: This is primarily a 180-degree CGI-animated film broken into three parts. My review is only based on the first part as that was what was available at the time. The story is a typical dystopian sci-fi fantasy with a young woman with a powerful gift and a secret about her past being kept from her… nothing about that stands out as particularly unique to the genre. I can’t speak to the rest of the story as yet, as it is not available as of this review.

There were some great visual effects that I would say are on par with some of the Final Fantasy animations, though I personally don’t love the way the female characters are outfitted, this feels like a look that is more at home in the 1990’s than in the 2020’s. 

I also have some privacy concerns as to why the app requires constant internet access to use it, especially given that you can download the episodes to the headset. There have been other apps/games that lately have been following in this trend where they gather your personal data/information (possibly every time you use it, hence the constant internet connection requirement). I know that there can be other reasons for requiring constant access to a user’s headset, but if so I feel the developers might want to explain what data is gathered and how it is used (and yes there is a lengthy terms of service and privacy notice on their website, but if I was designing something like this I would want to be as transparent as possible about what data I was collecting). Hopefully they can clear the air about this concern.

The User Interface issues made pausing and replay/fast-forward not an option which is strange for a film. The interactive element seemed to hold some fascinating potential, and I could see it being applied retroactively to titles like Fahrenheit or Heavy Rain to great effect.

Final Take:
My hesitation has me giving this a 3-star rating until privacy matters are cleared up and we see the final product; it is currently polished in some places, dated in others, and holds some interesting elements, though I am personally uncertain if Mulan 2125 will be worth the wait.
